reason: Response A reads as more genuinely Sonnet: it commits to a clear rhetorical stance up front, develops ideas with controlled, varied prose and concrete specifics (entasis, wabi-sabi, the branch growing toward light) woven into flowing argument, and ends on a single distilled insight without resorting to listy scaffolding. Response B leans on imitation tells of the style—heavy reliance on tricolon list-structures ("Think of... Think of... Think of..."), stacked parallel examples (Parthenon/sonata/Da Vinci), and a slightly formulaic "X earns trust, Y earns attention" symmetry that feels engineered rather than reasoned. B's hedge-then-pivot framing ("If forced to choose, asymmetry has the edge, because it contains symmetry as a possibility") is the kind of neat-but-shallow flourish a mimic produces, whereas A's negotiation theme is more organically earned.
